channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Multimodal Context
channel.description: Latest Multimodal Context news in Context Engineering

item.title: Multimodal Claim Extraction for Fact-Checking
item.description: arXiv:2604.16311v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Automated Fact-Checking (AFC) relies on claim extraction as a first step, yet existing methods largely overlook the multimodal nature of today's misinformation. Social media posts often combine short, informal text with images such as memes, screensho...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, context, arxiv, multimodal, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: GoCoMA: Hyperbolic Multimodal Representation Fusion for Large Language Model-Generated Code Attribution
item.description: arXiv:2604.16377v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) trained on massive code corpora are now increasingly capable of generating code that is hard to distinguish from human-written code. This raises practical concerns, including security vulnerabilities and licensing ambiguit...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, embedding, arxiv, multimodal, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: CGCMA: Conditionally-Gated Cross-Modal Attention for Event-Conditioned Asynchronous Fusion
item.description: arXiv:2604.16411v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study asynchronous alignment, a first-class multimodal learning setting in which a dense primary stream must be fused with sporadic external context whose value depends on when it arrives. Unlike standard multimodal benchmarks that assume structura...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, context, arxiv, multimodal, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Discordance-Aware Multimodal Framework with Multi-Agent Clinical Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2604.16333v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Knee osteoarthritis frequently exhibits discordance between structural damage observed in imaging and patient-reported symptoms such as pain. This mismatch complicates clinical interpretation and patient stratification and remains insufficiently model...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> embedding, arxiv, reasoning, multimodal, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
